reasoning: Tnfaip3 encodes the ubiquitin‑editing protein A20, a key negative regulator of the NF‑κB signaling pathway. When A20 is reduced by CRISPRi knockdown, the inhibitory brake on NF‑κB is released, resulting in heightened NF‑κB activity. NF‑κB drives the transcription of many inflammatory and immune‑responsive genes, among them cathepsin L (Ctsl), which has been reported to be up‑regulated in contexts of enhanced NF‑κB signaling. Consequently, loss of Tnfaip3 is expected to increase Ctsl expression.
